summary: First-finish smoke test of Daily Offline Task Scheduler. Backend CRUD, reorder, sessions, stats all green. Frontend hero, theme toggle (dark + ls), task creation modal, priority badge, duration, tags, recurring icon, completion toggle, TimerWidget (10:00 -> 09:58 tick verified), search/filter, edit prefill, delete, drag-handle, stats panel and localStorage offline persistence all verified end-to-end via Playwright against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
passed: GET /api/tasks -> 200 [] initially
POST /api/tasks creates task with all fields, returns id, completed=false, elapsed_seconds=0
PUT /api/tasks/{id} sets completed=true
POST /api/tasks/reorder -> {ok:true}
POST /api/sessions logs seconds/mode
GET /api/stats returns total_tasks/completed_tasks/completion_pct/seconds_today/total_sessions
DELETE /api/tasks/{id} -> {ok:true}; bogus id -> 404
Frontend loads, TODAY's GRIND header, online-status, theme-toggle-btn visible
Theme toggle adds 'dark' class to <html> and persists in localStorage (scheduler.theme.v1=dark)
task-form-modal opens, fields fill, modal-save-btn creates a card with priority badge, duration (10m), tag chips and daily icon when recurring
toggle-task-{id} applies line-through to title
start-timer-{id} opens timer-widget, timer-play-pause-btn ticks countdown 10:00 -> 09:58 in 2s
timer-reset-btn and timer-close-btn work
search-input filters cards by title/tag (1 match / 0 non-match)
filter-all/active/done-btn all clickable
edit-task-{id} reopens modal pre-filled; save updates card
drag-handle-{id} present and visible
Stats panel shows stat-completed, stat-progress, stat-time, stat-remaining values
localStorage 'scheduler.tasks.v1' populated; after page reload task card still renders (offline cache)
delete-task-{id} removes the card and shows 'Task removed' toast

failed.issue: After deleting the last task the [data-testid='empty-state'] block did not become visible (list area was empty but the empty-state element not rendered immediately after delete).
failed.evidence: page.locator('[data-testid="empty-state"]').is_visible() returned False right after delete (toast 'Task removed' was shown, no task cards present).

action_items: Optional: ensure EmptyState renders when filtered list becomes 0 (not only on initial load) so users see the friendly empty-state CTA after deleting their last task.
